level_signals: 6+ years of experience in product management or a customer-facing role
Comp range $216K-$270K base consistent with Senior IC at Scale AI
Owns roadmap for a full data vertical end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in product management or a customer-facing role.
Technical fluency: Software engineering background (a degree in computer science or equivalent experience).
Entrepreneurial mindset: A builder excited by ambiguity and motivated to create new products from the ground up.
Understanding of reinforcement learnings, simulation environments, or data pipelines for model training and evaluation
Strong customer intuition and the ability to translate technical requirements into impactful product decisions.
Bias for action and comfort wearing multiple hats and operating in fast-moving environments.

scope.surface: Agent & Reinforcement Learning Environments data vertical, with focus on Computer Using Agent (CUA) data
scope.business_model: B2B data foundry / AI training data products for frontier AI labs and enterprise customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Data quality and pipeline throughput for CUA vertical
Adoption and satisfaction of researcher-facing tooling at AI labs
cross_functional_partners: Research teams at leading AI labs
core_responsibilities: Own the roadmap for the Agent & RL Environment Data vertical, setting product direction and driving execution across engineering, operations, and go-to-market teams.
Build technical partnerships with research teams at leading AI labs, identifying insights that shape new product lines and competitive strategies for your vertical.
Design, experiment with, and deliver high-quality data pipelines, tooling, and evaluation frameworks that advance RL and agentic model capabilities.
Scope out and scale the creation of RL environments that simulate real-world use cases.
Collaborate cross-functionally, influencing business priorities and diving in the weeds of research, operations, and customer interactions.

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($216K-$270K base)
Clear product surface and ownership area (Agent & RL Environments / CUA data vertical)
Direct access to leading AI lab researchers and Scale's most important customers
High-impact domain at the frontier of AI development (agentic + RL)
red_flags: Role appears to require on-site presence in SF, NYC, or Seattle — no remote option stated
Visa sponsorship policy not addressed in the JD
Section header says 'Ideally, You'd Have' which implies nice-to-haves, but most bullets read as hard requirements (e.g. 6+ years, CS degree); treated as hard given unconditional language
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for the same role may deter reapplicants

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company context)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from pay transparency statement listing specific cities only)
ambiguities: All requirements appear under 'Ideally, You'd Have' which typically implies preferences, but the language for most items (6+ years, CS degree/equivalent, RL understanding) is unconditional and reads as hard requirements. Treated as must_haves with severity: hard. The softer items (entrepreneurial mindset, bias for action) are borderline cultural_fit but still placed in must_haves given the section framing.
Location type: the subtitle reference and pay transparency section list specific cities (SF, NY, Seattle) suggesting onsite, but explicit hybrid/remote policy is not stated. Treated as onsite.
Visa sponsorship: not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as unclear.
